title: Tolerance Range Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive bell curves showing specialist and generalist species tolerance ranges across environmental gradients, with draggable condition markers and zone highlighting

learningObjectives: Demonstrate how changes in environmental conditions affect organism survival by manipulating tolerance curves
Distinguish between specialist and generalist species based on tolerance range width
Predict which species will be more vulnerable to environmental change
prerequisites: Familiarity with environmental factors and abiotic conditions

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Draggable exploration with species comparison across environmental gradients
keyQuestions: Why do specialist species have narrower tolerance ranges than generalists?
At what environmental condition does one species begin to outperform the other?
How does climate change affect specialists differently than generalists?
Can a species be a specialist for one environmental variable and a generalist for another?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think specialists are 'better' than generalists, not understanding that each strategy has advantages in different environments
Students may assume that stress zones are always lethal, when organisms can survive in stress zones but with reduced fitness
Students sometimes think tolerance ranges are fixed, not realizing that acclimation and adaptation can shift them over time
assessmentOpportunities: Have students create data tables recording species zones at specific environmental values
Ask students to predict community composition changes under climate change scenarios
Students research a local species and sketch its tolerance curve for a key environmental variable
